Curriculum

What You'll Learn

Walk away with real, actionable knowledge — no fluff, no sales pitch.

01

How to buy a home without a bank or credit check

02

Understanding Rent-to-Own, Lease Options & Contract for Deed

03

How to qualify even if you've been denied by traditional lenders

04

The step-by-step process to get into a home in OKC

05

How to avoid common mistakes first-time alternative financing buyers make

06

Live Q&A with a licensed Oklahoma real estate agent

Real Families

Don't Just Take Our Word For It

Hear from Oklahoma families who found their home through our program.

Questions

Frequently Asked

Yes, 100% free. No hidden fees, no catch. We host this seminar to educate Oklahoma City families about alternative paths to homeownership. You won't be asked to pay anything.

No. That's exactly what this seminar is about. We'll teach you alternative financing options — Rent-to-Own, Lease Options, and Contract for Deed — that don't require traditional bank approval or a perfect credit score.

Just yourself and an open mind. We'll provide everything you need. Bring a notebook if you'd like to take notes. Light refreshments will be provided.

No. This is a genuine educational event. You'll leave with real, actionable knowledge about how to buy a home without a bank. No pressure, no hard sells — just information you can use.

The event is in Oklahoma City. The exact address and directions will be sent to you via email and text after you register. This ensures we can manage capacity and provide you with parking details.

Absolutely. Buying a home is a family decision, and we want everyone involved to hear the information firsthand. Just make sure they register too so we can save their seat.

Still have questions?

Your seat is free.
We answer everything at the event.

Don't miss it — only 50 spots available.

Se Habla Español

This is not legal or financial advice. Tez is a licensed real estate agent in Oklahoma, not an attorney or financial advisor. Home Run Homes is a matchmaking service connecting buyers with owner-financed properties. Always read agreements before signing and consult with appropriate professionals.
